title: canthium scandens
intro: Canthium scandens, also known as the "Wild Coffee" or "Ivory Coast Coffee", is a climbing shrub native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other ailments. The leaves and roots of Canthium scandens have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing pain and improving wound healing. Canthium scandens is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and has also been used to make a medicinal coffee substitute, particularly in West African traditional medicine.
